mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases (including reported FLT3 activity) to block proliferative signaling
additional_notes: Noted to include FLT3 activity; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Inhibits multiple receptor tyrosine kinases involved in growth/angiogenic signaling
additional_notes: Mentioned as multikinase with reported AML activity in some contexts; range given as 1 nM–10 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Inhibits SRC-family and ABL kinases to disrupt leukemia-associated signaling pathways
additional_notes: Range given as 0.1 nM–1 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 to suppress cytokine-driven JAK-STAT signaling and proliferation in dependent subsets
additional_notes: Range given as 1 nM–10 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway to reduce downstream ERK signaling and proliferation
Inhibits anti-apoptotic BCL-2 and BCL-XL to trigger apoptosis
additional_notes: Clinical toxicity concerns noted; range given as 0.3 nM–3 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Disrupts microtubule formation via tubulin interference, impairing cell division
additional_notes: Range given as 3 nM–30 µM (mixed units); described as tubulin disruption with cancer reports.

mechanism: Induces cytotoxic stress via ALDH inhibition and metal-dependent effects (often enhanced with copper)
additional_notes: Text notes ALDH/metal-dependent cytotoxicity and that effects are often with Cu; range given as 10 nM–10 µM (mixed units) and recommends predefined Cu dosing/controls.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases, altering transcription and promoting differentiation programs in AML contexts
additional_notes: Mentioned as used in AML differentiation regimens.

mechanism: Potent HDAC inhibition leading to transcriptional reprogramming and anti-proliferative effects
Activates retinoic acid receptor signaling to drive myeloid differentiation (classically in APL)
additional_notes: Alternative name/acronym: ATRA; note warns proliferation IC50 may miss differentiation effects and suggests CD11b/flow as secondary; range given as 1 nM–10 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Inhibits mitochondrial complex I, reducing oxidative phosphorylation and indirectly limiting proliferation in some AML settings
additional_notes: Described as mitochondrial complex I with indirect antiproliferative effects.

mechanism: Disrupts mitochondrial electron transport to impair cellular energy metabolism
additional_notes: Described as mitochondrial electron transport; anticancer screens.

mechanism: Mechanism noted as context-dependent; reported to have anti-cancer cell effects in multiple studies
additional_notes: Range given as 3 nM–10 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Reported to affect WNT/STAT3 signaling and mitochondrial uncoupling, reducing proliferation/survival
additional_notes: Mechanism notes: WNT/STAT3/mitochondrial uncoupling; range given as 10 nM–10 µM (mixed units).

mechanism: Raises lysosomal pH and inhibits autophagy/lysosomal function, often used as a sensitizer
additional_notes: Text lists as 'Chloroquine or Hydroxychloroquine' and notes often used as sensitizer.
